description: Create a content slide on the expected impact of AI on risk management roles using a two-column layout with a left-side horizontal bar chart and narrative bullets, plus a right sidebar quote.

header.headline: Expected impact of AI on evolution of risk management roles in next three years

left.body:
AI has the potential to transform some roles, particularly those with primarily manual workloads (e.g., compliance testing, reporting, controls, data analysis). But it will also create entirely new roles and reshape others, as our results highlight:
• 55% say the creation of hybrid AI-risk specialist roles is a priority.
• 51% of respondents expect to hire more specialists (e.g., cyber, climate, quantum, AI governance).
• 31% believe AI will enable a restructuring of risk resources.
To navigate these shifts, 79% of CROs say they will emphasize upskilling in AI and data science, with more training in data analytics, model interpretation and AI tools.

main.title: Figure 25: Expected impact of AI on evolution of risk management roles in next three years

series.label: AI/data science upskilling – greater investment in training staff to use data analytics, model interpretation and AI tools
Reduction in traditionally manual roles – automation reducing roles that follow routine processes (e.g., compliance testing, reporting, controls, data analysis)
Hybrid AI-risk specialist roles – emergence of risk professionals with both domain knowledge and AI proficiency
AI vendor and service provider reliance – increasing dependence on third-party AI platforms, services and consulting partners
Ethical and model governance roles – introduction of new positions focused on AI explainability, fairness, compliance and regulatory oversight
AI talent recruitment/retention – greater challenges hiring and retaining AI-capable staff (e.g., wage inflation, sustainability of workforce/skills)

sidebar.title: What CROs say:
sidebar.body: From a staffing perspective, the trajectory is toward more skilled, highly paid, strategic professionals, with not as many worker bees.
